title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:1
body: When the sun is folded away enfolded and stripped of its light

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:10
body: and when the scrolls the scrolls containing the deeds are unrolled read nushirat or nushshirat when they are unfolded and spread out;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:11
body: and when the heaven is stripped off torn away from its place just as the skin of a sheep is stripped off;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:12
body: and when the Hell-fire is set ablaze read su‘irat or su‘‘irat when it is set alight;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:13
body: and when Paradise is brought near when it is brought close to those who have merited it that they may enter it the response to idhā ‘when’ at the beginning of the sūra and all that has been supplemented thereto is the following

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:14
body: then a soul every soul at the time of the occurrence of the things mentioned which is the Day of Resurrection will know what it has presented of good or evil.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:15
body: So I swear fa-lā lā is extra by the receding planets

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:16
body: the movers the setters that is the five planets Saturn Jupiter Mars Venus and Mercury takhnusu means ‘they sink away’ during their orbit whereas we see stars at the end of their zodiacal course return to the start; taknisu means that they enter their kinās ‘hideaway’ in other words they retreat into their setting-places;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:17
body: and by the night as it approaches as it falls with its darkness or it means as it recedes;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:18
body: and by the dawn as it breathes as it extends until it becomes broad daylight

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:19
body: truly this Qur’ān is the word of a messenger who is noble in the sight of God exalted be He — this being the messenger Gabriel it qawl ‘word’ has been annexed to him because he descends with it

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:2
body: and when the stars scatter when they are extinguished and hurtle down towards the earth

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:20
body: powerful one of mighty powers eminent one of eminent status makīnin is semantically connected ‘inda ‘in the presence of’ in the presence of the Lord of the Throne that is God exalted be He;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:21
body: obeyed there the angels obey him in the heavens trustworthy with the Revelation.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:22
body: And your companion Muhammad (s) wa-mā sāhibukum a supplement to the clause from innahu ‘truly this is’ up to the last of the oath clauses is not a madman as you claim.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:23
body: For verily he Muhammad (s) saw him Gabriel in the form in which he was created on the clear horizon the highest one towards the east;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:24
body: and he Muhammad (s) is not to be accused of knowing the Unseen what is hidden of the revelation and the tidings of the heaven a variant reading for bi-zanīnin has bi-danīnin meaning that he is not ‘niggardly’ with the Unseen so as to reduce something of it and not disclose it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:25
body: And it that is the Qur’ān is not the word of an accursed satan eavesdropping stealthily.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:26
body: So where are you going? So what path are you going to follow in repudiating the Qur’ān and turning away from it?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:27
body: It is only a reminder an admonition for all worlds those of mankind and jinn

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:28
body: for those of you who li-man shā’a minkum is a substitution for li’l-‘ālamīna ‘for all worlds’ using the same preposition wish to go straight by following the truth;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:29
body: but you will not wish to go straight along the path of truth unless God the Lord of the Worlds of all creatures wills that you should go straight along it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:3
body: and when the mountains are set in motion when they are blown away from the face of the earth becoming as scattered dust

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:4
body: and when the pregnant camels are neglected abandoned without a herdsman or anyone to milk them on account of the matter that has come over them — even though previously no wealth was dearer to them than these pregnant camels;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:5
body: and when the wild beasts are mustered when they are brought together after being resurrected so that each of them may retaliate against the other before becoming dust;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:6
body: and when the seas are set afire read sujirat or sujjirat when they are set alight and become a mass of fire;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:7
body: and when the souls are coupled when they are paired up with their bodies;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:8
body: and when the girl buried-alive the young girl buried alive for fear of shame and impoverishment asks — in rebuke of its slayer —

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 81:9
body: for what sin she was slain sa’alat ‘asks’ a variant reading has su’ilat ‘she is asked’ narrating the address directed to her to which she then responds by saying ‘I was slain without having committed any sin’;
